perturbation.original_text: 1.10 "Formulation" means a specific combination of excipient(s) that can formulate the Active Ingredient, as well as compounds other than the Active Ingredient, developed as a result of the work conducted under the Development Plan.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10 "Formulation" means the general concept of mixing ingredients.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Formulation' specifies it as 'a specific combination' developed under the Development Plan. The modified definition broadens it to 'the general concept of mixing ingredients,' removing the specificity and connection to the Development Plan. This creates a contradiction because later sections rely on the original specific definition, particularly concerning intellectual property and regulatory approvals, which become ambiguous with a generic definition. For instance, Section 3.4 discusses CARDAX's use of CAPSUGEL's confidential information in connection with seeking regulatory approval for a Compound Formulation or the Product. With the changed definition, it's unclear what constitutes a Compound Formulation, leading to uncertainty in enforcement and potentially impacting regulatory compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CARDAX may use the confidential information of CAPSUGEL generated under this Agreement, except for CAPSUGEL's internal technical protocols and Background IP, to the extent necessary (i) in connection with seeking regulatory approval for a Compound Formulation or the Product and/or (ii) filing a patent application. "Compound Formulation" means any specific combination of excipient(s) and the Active Ingredient developed as a result of the work conducted under this Agreement. CARDAX may use and disclose CAPSUGEL's internal technical protocols and Background IP, to the extent necessary for Regulatory Approvals as contemplated by Section 2.3(b) to the extent reasonably determined by CAPSUGEL after notice and consultation with CAPSUGEL by CARDAX.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Formulation' broadens it to 'the general concept of mixing ingredients,' removing the specificity and connection to the Development Plan, whereas the contradicted text from Section 3.4 defines 'Compound Formulation' as a 'specific combination of excipient(s) and the Active Ingredient developed as a result of the work conducted under this Agreement.' This removes the specificity required for CARDAX's regulatory approval and patent application processes, and therefore creates an in-text contradiction. This adheres to the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1.26 "Specifications" shall mean the Product description and attributes agreed upon between the Parties upon conclusion of the Development Plan and appended to this Agreement as Exhibit B that will be attached hereto and, when attached, will be a part hereof, prior to commercialization of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.26 "Specifications" shall mean the initial Product description.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Specifications' ties it to a mutually agreed-upon detailed description finalized after the Development Plan and documented in Exhibit B. The modified definition simplifies it to 'the initial Product description,' removing the crucial element of mutual agreement, the Development Plan's influence, and Exhibit B. This change creates an internal contradiction because subsequent clauses, such as Section 8.1(b) regarding CAPSUGEL's indemnification of CARDAX, hinge on compliance with the defined 'Specifications'. If the specifications are merely the 'initial' ones, the basis for determining compliance and liability becomes unclear. The removal of Exhibit B, where the specifications are supposed to be written, creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CAPSUGEL shall indemnify, defend and hold CARDAX, its Affiliates and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents harmless from and against any and all third party Losses suffered, incurred or sustained by CARDAX or to which CARDAX becomes subject at any time, to the extent arising out of or resulting, directly or indirectly, from: (a) any breach of CAPSUGEL's representations, warranties or obligations under this Agreement; (b) any personal injury, death or property damage caused by the possession, use, or consumption by any person of any Product that does not comply with the Specification in any way or is the result of actions or inactions of CAPSUGEL in its manufacturing or is alleged to result from any inherent risk of the Formulation or a defect in the Formulation; and (c) any other negligent act or omission on the part of CAPSUGEL, its Affiliates or their respective employees or agents except, in each case, to the extent such claims are attributable to the gross negligence or willful misconduct of CARDAX.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Specifications" as 'the initial Product description' directly contradicts Section 8.1(b) because that section explicitly links CAPSUGEL's indemnification obligations to Products that do 'not comply with the Specification'. By removing the requirement that the specifications be mutually agreed upon and documented in Exhibit B, the contract no longer has a clear and agreed-upon standard for determining compliance. This introduces ambiguity and makes it difficult to enforce CAPSUGEL's indemnification obligations, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. The original definition ties 'Specifications' to Exhibit B which is no longer referenced, creating a contradiction between the modified text and the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Section 2 Product Development, Manufacture and Commercialization 2.1 Governance Process Among the Parties . Both CARDAX and CAPSUGEL will agree upon a development plan, which shall be in writing and attached hereto as Exhibit A (the "Development Plan"), which shall describe various parameters including each Party's duties, obligations, time schedule and deliverables schedule. The activities performed under the Development Plan (the "Development Activities") shall be administered by a joint project team ("JPT"), which shall review/update/amend the Development Plan for the Product in the Territory and coordinate the Formulation, development, manufacturing and commercialization of the Product, including identifying and selecting one or more Marketers as contemplated under Section 2.4. Each Party shall appoint a project manager to oversee that Party's performance of its obligations under this Agreement and shall notify the other Party of the name and full contact details of its appointed project manager. The JPT shall comply with this Agreement for decisions specifically assigned to a Party pursuant to this Agreement. Meetings shall take place by telephone or in person and the JPT will operate by consensus. If consensus cannot be reached, the matter will be submitted to the Head of Dosage Form Solutions of CAPSUGEL and the President and CEO of CARDAX for resolution. If such matter is not resolved, then the Parties may attempt to mediate such issue under the JAMS mediation rules. No member or any Affiliate of any member of the JPT shall have any liability under this Agreement and shall be exculpated to the fullest extent not prohibited by law from any liability to any Party that such member is not an employee, officer, consultant or acting in any similar capacity.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2 Product Development, Manufacture and Commercialization 2.1 Governance Process Among the Parties . CAPSUGEL will lead the product development.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 2.1 describes a joint governance process through a Development Plan (Exhibit A) managed by a joint project team (JPT), requiring mutual agreement and consensus. The modified text significantly alters this by stating 'CAPSUGEL will lead the product development,' removing any explicit mention of a Development Plan, JPT, or CARDAX's shared control. This creates an internal contradiction because other sections still reference the Development Plan, Development Activities, and shared responsibilities, as contemplated in the original Section 2.1. For example, Section 2.2 CAPSUGEL Responsibilities states CAPSUGEL will perform the development work necessary to formulate, analytically develop and take all other developmental actions necessary or required to develop the Product and manufacture pre- clinical and clinical batches (collectively, the 'CAPSUGEL Development Activities'). This Section is now unclear since the definition of Development Actitivies mentioned is removed. Similarly, Section 3.6 mentions each party will jointly own all inventions and other Intellectual Property Rights jointly made under this Agreement that are directly resulting from work conducted under this Agreement in accordance with the Development Plan and related specifically to the Product or the Compound Formulation, including any patents, patent applications and other Intellectual Property Rights related to such inventions, if any, unless otherwise expressly set forth herein. The removal of Section 2.1, creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 CAPSUGEL Responsibilities. With respect to the Product, CAPSUGEL, [***] shall [***] perform the development work necessary to formulate, analytically develop and take all other developmental actions necessary or required to develop the Product and manufacture pre- clinical and clinical batches (collectively, the "CAPSUGEL Development Activities" ). For purposes of further clarification, CAPSUGEL Development Activities shall include, without limitation, each of the following performed with all due diligence, care and skill and in accordance with all other Applicable Laws:
perturbation.justification: The changed text, which states 'CAPSUGEL will lead the product development,' directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 2.2) because Section 2.2 references 'CAPSUGEL Development Activities,' a term that is originally defined within the original version of Section 2.1, which contemplates a joint effort. By removing the joint effort and the term's definition, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction with the continued use of the term 'CAPSUGEL Development Activities' in Section 2.2 without a clear definition. This omission introduces ambiguity and inconsistency, thus creating a strong contradiction.
